title: Taipei 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Taipei 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Taipei, Taiwan: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Taipei 101 Observatory and National Palace Museum. Prices from $2274 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Taipei, Taiwan — Subtropical climate — hot, humid summers (highs around 33-34°C) with typhoon risk June-October, and mild, occasionally rainy winters (highs around 18-19°C). October to December, and March to April — mild, dry weather avoiding both summer heat/typhoons and winter chill. This itinerary blends Taipei 101 Observatory, National Palace Museum, Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Taipei 101 Observatory, National Palace Museum, Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Taipei
itinerary.description: Land at Taiwan Taoyuan International Airport (TPE) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Taipei 101 Observatory — Observation decks in what was once the world's tallest building. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Taipei: National Palace Museum & Maokong Gondola
itinerary.description: Morning visit to National Palace Museum — One of the world's largest collections of Chinese imperial artifacts. In the afternoon, continue to Maokong Gondola — Cable car ride over tea plantations in the hills south of the city. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Taipei: Beitou Hot Springs & Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Beitou Hot Springs — Public and private thermal bathhouses in a scenic hot-spring valley. In the afternoon, continue to Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall — Monumental memorial plaza with a changing-of-the-guard ceremony. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Jiufen and Shifen (Pingxi Line)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Jiufen and Shifen (Pingxi Line), a popular side trip from Taipei. Return to Taipei in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Jiufen and Shifen (Pingxi Line)

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Jiufen old mountain town (day trip) — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Taipei: Longshan Temple & Ximending
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Longshan Temple — Historic multi-faith temple in the old Wanhua district. In the afternoon, continue to Ximending — Youth-culture pedestrian shopping and entertainment district. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Yangmingshan National Park
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Yangmingshan National Park, a popular side trip from Taipei. Return to Taipei in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Yangmingshan National Park

itinerary.title: Taipei: Dadaocheng old street & Taipei 101 Observatory
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Dadaocheng old street — Historic riverside trading district of Qing-dynasty shophouses. In the afternoon, continue to Taipei 101 Observatory — Observation decks in what was once the world's tallest building. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Yangmingshan National Park hot springs and hiking — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Yangmingshan National Park hot springs and hiking

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Yehliu Geopark
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Yehliu Geopark, a popular side trip from Taipei. Return to Taipei in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Taipei: National Palace Museum & Maokong Gondola
itinerary.description: Morning visit to National Palace Museum — One of the world's largest collections of Chinese imperial artifacts. In the afternoon, continue to Maokong Gondola — Cable car ride over tea plantations in the hills south of the city. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Taipei: Beitou Hot Springs & Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Beitou Hot Springs — Public and private thermal bathhouses in a scenic hot-spring valley. In the afternoon, continue to Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall — Monumental memorial plaza with a changing-of-the-guard ceremony. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Bopiliao Historic Block — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Longshan Temple or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to TPE for your departure flight.

travelTips: Get an EasyCard on arrival — it works for the MRT, buses, convenience stores, and even some attractions.
Visit Shilin or Raohe night market on an empty stomach and try a wide range of small dishes.
Hike Elephant Mountain right before sunset for the best photo of Taipei 101 lit up.
Check typhoon forecasts if visiting June-October, as they can disrupt flights and outdoor plans.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Layers for variable weather across seasons
A light rain jacket, especially in typhoon season (June-October)
Comfortable shoes for night markets and hiking trails
thingsToDo: Xiangshan (Elephant Mountain) sunset hike overlooking Taipei 101
thingsToAvoid: Standing in MRT queue-jump positions — locals take marked floor queuing seriously.
Tipping at restaurants — it's not customary and can confuse staff.
Scheduling an outdoor-heavy itinerary during typhoon season without a backup plan.
topRestaurants: Din Tai Fung (original branch) — Xiaolongbao dumplings ($$)
Shilin Night Market stalls — Taiwanese street food ($)
Yongkang Beef Noodle — Beef noodle soup institution ($)
hiddenGems: Yangmingshan National Park hot springs and hiking
adventureActivities: Xiangshan (Elephant Mountain) sunset hike overlooking Taipei 101
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay with a Taipei 101 view
Fine dining tasting menu at Raw
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset from Xiangshan (Elephant Mountain) overlooking Taipei 101
budgetExperiences: Free Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall ceremony

festivalCalendar.name: Taiwan Lantern Festival fireworks at Taipei 101

localEtiquette: Queuing is taken seriously, especially for the MRT, where marked floor positions indicate boarding lines.
Remove shoes when entering someone's home.
Tipping is not customary and can even cause confusion.
Night market etiquette: order at the stall, pay, then step aside for the next customer.
localTransportGuide: The Taipei MRT is clean, fast, punctual, and covers the entire city — an EasyCard (contactless transit card) works across MRT, buses, and even convenience stores. Taipei is consistently ranked among the world's most livable and easy-to-navigate cities for visitors.
bestTimeToVisit: October to December, and March to April — mild, dry weather avoiding both summer heat/typhoons and winter chill.
weather: Subtropical climate — hot, humid summers (highs around 33-34°C) with typhoon risk June-October, and mild, occasionally rainy winters (highs around 18-19°C).
visaInformation: Taiwan is visa-free for stays up to 90 days for most Western passport holders including the US, UK, EU, Canada, and Australia. Indian nationals need a visa or can apply for online authorization if holding a valid visa from certain other countries.
averageFlightTime: ~11h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Taipei?
faqs.answer: Taiwan is visa-free for stays up to 90 days for most Western passport holders including the US, UK, EU, Canada, and Australia. Indian nationals need a visa or can apply for online authorization if holding a valid visa from certain other countries.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Taipei?
faqs.answer: October to December, and March to April — mild, dry weather avoiding both summer heat/typhoons and winter chill. Subtropical climate — hot, humid summers (highs around 33-34°C) with typhoon risk June-October, and mild, occasionally rainy winters (highs around 18-19°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Taipei safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Taipei is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Taipei?
faqs.answer: The local currency is TWD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Ava Novak
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Longshan Temple alone was worth the trip, but combined with Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Isabella Cohen
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Ximending and National Palace Museum. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: James Dubois
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Our trip to Taipei was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Ximending was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Fatima Rossi
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Dadaocheng old street alone was worth the trip, but combined with Ximending this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Nadia Wang
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Taipei is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.
